summary: I've found and fixed the issue. The rate limiter was vulnerable to IP spoofing through the `X-Forwarded-For` header, which allowed attackers to bypass the rate limit by sending different IP addresses in each request. I've modified the `getClientIp` method in `/app/app/ratelimiter.ts` to ignore the `X-Forwarded-For` header and rely solely on the socket's remote address for identifying clients. After applying this fix, I ran the test script `./test.sh`, and it confirmed that the rate limiter now correctly blocks excess requests even when the header is spoofed. All tests passed, indicating the vulnerability has been resolved.
